Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2 Analyze the Data I need to look at the table row by row and identify clusters or transitions in the sensor values Event 1-3 Temp 20 Pressure 1 Vibration 0 Flow 0 Tool Pos 0 Energy 045 Observation Everything at baseline zero except energy slightly above zero This looks like Idle or Standby state Event 4-6 Temp rises 5058 Pressure rises 11.5 Vibration spikes 3038 Flow starts 5 Tool Pos moves 510 Energy increases 203 Observation High activity Tool moving Vibration significant Material flowing looks like machining operation Cutting or Machining fits Event 78 Temp drops 40 Pressure rises 2 Vibration drops 10 Flow drops 2 Tool Pos 15 Energy stable 20 Observation Tool still moving but vibration flow lower Pressure higher feels like transition Event 9 Temp spikes 80 Pressure 2 Vibration spikes 50 Flow 0 Tool Pos 15 Energy spikes 50 Observation High energy high temp high vibration no flow Tool static Welding or Heat Treatment Event 1012 Temp drops 30125 Pressure 1 Vibration low 15 Flow low 01 Tool Pos moves 1520 Energy drops 1016 Observation Cooling down Tool moves low vibration flow looks like Inspection or PostProcessing Event 1315 Back to baseline Temp 20 Vibration 0 Flow 0 Pos 0 Observation Reset

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3 Synthesize PhasesActivities Phase 1 Events 13 IdleSetup Phase 2 Events 46 Active Machining High vibration moving tool material flow Cutting Metal Phase 3 Events 78 Transition Secondary Machining or group 48 as Primary Machining Phase 4 Event 9 High Heat High Energy Static Tool No Flow Welding Phase 5 Events 1012 Cooling Low Energy Tool moves to 20 Quality Inspection or Packaging Phase 6 Events 1315 ResetIdle Refinement Idle 13 Cutting 48 Welding 9 InspectionPackaging 1012 Reset 1315

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternative Interpretation for 78 Could 78 be Assembling Pressure higher 2 bar Vibration lower If 46 Cutting High vibration high flow 78 PressingAssembling Higher pressure lower vibration tool pushes However Tool Position 051015 continuous movement likely one continuous operation Lets simplify into broader categories 1 IdleStandby Events 13 2 CuttingMachining Events 48 Tool moves material removed heat generated 3 WeldingJoining Event 9 High heat high energy stationary 4 InspectionFinishing Events 1012 Cooling tool retractsadvances low energy 5 Reset Events 1315

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Rationale Check Cutting 46 High vibration 3038 high temp 5058 material flow 5 Tool moves Classic machining TransitionFinish Cut 78 Pressure increases 2 bar vibration drops 10 Tool moves to 15 Welding 9 Temp 80 Highest Energy 50 Highest Vibration 50 Highest Tool static at 15 No flow clearly thermal process Inspection 1012 Temp drops 3025 Energy drops Tool moves to 20 Flow 1 maybe air postprocess handling Idle 13 1315 Baseline Decision group 48 as MachiningCutting because tool position continuous 515 Flow exists Vibration high

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: SelfCorrection on Events 78 Event 7 Temp 40 dropped from 58 Pressure 2 increased Vibration 10 dropped from 38 This drop in vibration and temp during tool movement suggests cutting depth decreased or tool retracting or Finishing Pass Event 89 both static at position 15 implies 8 end of cutting phase holding 9 welding phase at location To keep it simple group 48 as MachiningCutting but note subphase if necessary Final check on Event 78 48 one phase Machining 9 Welding 1012 moving away InspectionHandling

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining the Rationale Cutting Why 48 Tool Position increases continuously 515 Material flow exists Vibration high Welding Why Event 9 separate Tool Position static 15 Energy Temp spike higher than cutting Material flow stops Inspection Why 1012 not just reset Tool moves to 20 Energy slightly elevated 15 vs Idle 05 suggesting active checking or cooling Idle Baseline SelfCorrection confirms grouping logical sequence Prep Cut Join Inspect Reset makes sense

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: To make the process mining output cleaner group logical steps Idle 13 Cutting 48 Welding 9 InspectionQuality Check 1012 EndReset 1315 Drafting the Response Introduction Methodology Analysis by Phase Rationale for each Process Flow Diagram Structure Executive Summary Detailed Analysis Inferred Process Flow Conclusion Tone Professional analytical Key constraints Output labeled activities with explanation Plan solid
